power_monitoring.title: Power Monitoring
power_monitoring.description: Select the sensors that monitor your solar production and power flow. These are typically provided by your inverter integration (e.g., SolarEdge, Fronius, Enphase).

data_description.solar_entity: Sensor showing current solar panel production in watts (e.g., sensor.solaredge_current_power)
data_description.grid_entity: Sensor showing grid power flow. Positive = importing from grid, Negative = exporting to grid
data_description.home_load_entity: Sensor showing total home power consumption excluding EV charging. If not available, leave empty and it will be calculated.

battery.title: Home Battery (Optional)
battery.description: Configure home battery settings. Skip if you don't have a battery. AmpPilot will wait for the battery to reach the minimum SoC before using excess solar for EV charging.

data.min_battery_soc: Minimum battery SoC before EV charging (%)

data_description.battery_soc_entity: Sensor showing home battery charge level as percentage (e.g., sensor.powerwall_charge)
data_description.battery_power_entity: Sensor showing battery charging/discharging power. Positive = charging, Negative = discharging
data_description.min_battery_soc: EV charging won't start until home battery reaches this level. Default: 80%

vehicle_1.title: Vehicle 1
vehicle_1.description: Configure your first EV charger. The switch and amperage control entities are required. For Tesla vehicles, use vehicle sensors (sensor.X_charger_current, sensor.X_charging) instead of charger sensors for accurate power readings.

data_description.vehicle_name: Friendly name for this vehicle (e.g., 'Model Y', 'Phoenix'). Used in dashboard and logs.
data_description.charger_switch_entity: Switch to start/stop charging. For Tesla: switch.X_charge (vehicle) or switch.tesla_wall_connector_charging (TWC)
data_description.charger_amps_entity: Number entity to set charging amperage. For Tesla: number.X_charge_current (vehicle) or number.tesla_wall_connector_charging_current (TWC)
data_description.vehicle_connected_entity: Binary sensor showing if vehicle is plugged in (e.g., binary_sensor.X_charger)
data_description.charger_power_entity: Power sensor from the charger hardware. May not work with Mobile Connector - use vehicle amps sensor instead.
data_description.vehicle_charging_amps_entity: Current sensor from the vehicle itself (e.g., sensor.X_charger_current). More reliable than charger power for Mobile Connector.
data_description.vehicle_charging_state_entity: Charging state sensor from the vehicle (e.g., sensor.X_charging). Reports 'charging', 'stopped', 'disconnected', etc.
data_description.vehicle_soc_entity: Battery state of charge from the vehicle (e.g., sensor.X_battery). Used for display and future charge limiting.
data_description.min_amps: Minimum amperage the charger supports. Most EVs require at least 5-6A to charge. Default: 5A
data_description.max_amps: Maximum amperage for this charger. Limited by your circuit breaker and charger capacity. Default: 16A
data_description.phases: Number of electrical phases (1 for single-phase, 3 for three-phase). Affects power calculation.
data_description.voltage: Voltage per phase. Typically 230-240V for single-phase, 400V for three-phase. Default: 230V

vehicle_2.title: Vehicle 2 (Optional)
vehicle_2.description: Configure your second EV charger. Skip if you only have one EV. For Tesla vehicles, use vehicle sensors (sensor.X_charger_current, sensor.X_charging) instead of charger sensors for accurate power readings.

data_description.vehicle_name: Friendly name for this vehicle (e.g., 'Model 3', 'Slater'). Used in dashboard and logs.
data_description.charger_switch_entity: Switch to start/stop charging. For Tesla: switch.X_charge (vehicle) or switch.tesla_wall_connector_charging (TWC)
data_description.charger_amps_entity: Number entity to set charging amperage. For Tesla: number.X_charge_current (vehicle) or number.tesla_wall_connector_charging_current (TWC)
data_description.vehicle_connected_entity: Binary sensor showing if vehicle is plugged in (e.g., binary_sensor.X_charger)
data_description.charger_power_entity: Power sensor from the charger hardware. May not work with Mobile Connector - use vehicle amps sensor instead.
data_description.vehicle_charging_amps_entity: Current sensor from the vehicle itself (e.g., sensor.X_charger_current). More reliable than charger power for Mobile Connector.
data_description.vehicle_charging_state_entity: Charging state sensor from the vehicle (e.g., sensor.X_charging). Reports 'charging', 'stopped', 'disconnected', etc.
data_description.vehicle_soc_entity: Battery state of charge from the vehicle (e.g., sensor.X_battery). Used for display and future charge limiting.
data_description.min_amps: Minimum amperage the charger supports. Most EVs require at least 5-6A to charge. Default: 5A
data_description.max_amps: Maximum amperage for this charger. Limited by your circuit breaker and charger capacity. Default: 16A
data_description.phases: Number of electrical phases (1 for single-phase, 3 for three-phase). Affects power calculation.
data_description.voltage: Voltage per phase. Typically 230-240V for single-phase, 400V for three-phase. Default: 230V

distribution.title: Power Distribution
distribution.description: Configure how solar surplus is distributed when both vehicles are connected and ready to charge.

data_description.distribution_mode: Simultaneous Split: Divide surplus evenly between both vehicles. Priority Then Split: Fill priority vehicle first, then share remainder. Priority Only: Only charge the priority vehicle.
data_description.priority_vehicle: Which vehicle gets charging priority when using 'Priority' distribution modes.
data_description.min_surplus_per_vehicle: Minimum surplus watts needed to start charging each vehicle. Should cover minimum amps × voltage (e.g., 5A × 230V = 1150W).

thresholds.title: Charging Thresholds
thresholds.description: Configure when to start and stop solar charging. These settings prevent rapid on/off cycling caused by cloud cover or temporary load changes.

data_description.enable_threshold: Minimum surplus watts before starting EV charging. Should cover minimum amps (e.g., 6A × 230V = 1380W). Default: 1400W
data_description.enable_delay: How long surplus must stay above enable threshold before starting charge. Prevents starting on brief sunny moments. Default: 60s
data_description.disable_threshold: Stop charging when surplus drops below this. Use 0 to continue until grid import starts. Default: 0W
data_description.disable_delay: How long to wait below disable threshold before stopping. Prevents stopping on brief clouds. Default: 180s
data_description.household_buffer: Extra watts reserved for household appliances turning on. Prevents grid import spikes. Default: 200W

schedule.title: Scheduled Charging (Optional)
schedule.description: Configure time-based charging windows. When in 'Scheduled' mode, charging will only occur during these times at maximum amperage.

data_description.enable_scheduled: Enable the scheduled charging feature. Can be activated via the Charging Mode selector.
data_description.schedule_start: Time when scheduled charging window begins (24-hour format, e.g., 23:00 for 11 PM).
data_description.schedule_end: Time when scheduled charging window ends (24-hour format, e.g., 06:00 for 6 AM).
data_description.schedule_days: Days of the week when scheduled charging is active. Select multiple days for weekday/weekend patterns.

vehicle_1.title: Vehicle 1
vehicle_1.description: Configure your first EV charger. For Tesla vehicles, use vehicle sensors for accurate readings.

data_description.vehicle_name: Friendly name for this vehicle (e.g., 'Model Y', 'Phoenix').
data_description.charger_switch_entity: Switch to start/stop charging. For Tesla: switch.X_charge
data_description.charger_amps_entity: Number entity to set amperage. For Tesla: number.X_charge_current
data_description.vehicle_connected_entity: Binary sensor showing if vehicle is plugged in.
data_description.charger_power_entity: Power sensor from charger. May not work with Mobile Connector.
data_description.vehicle_charging_amps_entity: Current sensor from vehicle (sensor.X_charger_current). More reliable for Mobile Connector.
data_description.vehicle_charging_state_entity: Charging state from vehicle (sensor.X_charging). Reports 'charging', 'stopped', etc.
data_description.min_amps: Minimum amps the charger supports. Default: 5A
data_description.max_amps: Maximum amps for this charger. Default: 16A

vehicle_2.title: Vehicle 2 (Optional)
vehicle_2.description: Configure your second EV charger. Leave switch/amps empty to disable.

data_description.charger_switch_entity: Switch to start/stop charging. For Tesla: switch.X_charge
data_description.charger_amps_entity: Number entity to set amperage. For Tesla: number.X_charge_current
data_description.vehicle_connected_entity: Binary sensor showing if vehicle is plugged in.
data_description.charger_power_entity: Power sensor from charger. May not work with Mobile Connector.
data_description.vehicle_charging_amps_entity: Current sensor from vehicle (sensor.X_charger_current). More reliable for Mobile Connector.
data_description.vehicle_charging_state_entity: Charging state from vehicle (sensor.X_charging). Reports 'charging', 'stopped', etc.
data_description.min_amps: Minimum amps the charger supports. Default: 5A
data_description.max_amps: Maximum amps for this charger. Default: 16A

distribution.title: Power Distribution
distribution.description: Configure how solar surplus is distributed when both vehicles are connected.

data_description.distribution_mode: Simultaneous Split: divide evenly. Priority Then Split: fill priority first. Priority Only: only charge priority vehicle.
data_description.min_surplus_per_vehicle: Minimum surplus watts needed to start charging each vehicle.

thresholds.title: Charging Thresholds
thresholds.description: Configure when to start and stop solar charging.

data_description.enable_threshold: Minimum surplus watts before starting EV charging. Default: 1400W
data_description.enable_delay: Seconds to wait above threshold before starting. Default: 60s
data_description.disable_threshold: Stop charging when surplus drops below this. Default: 0W
data_description.disable_delay: Seconds to wait below threshold before stopping. Default: 180s
data_description.household_buffer: Extra watts reserved for appliances. Default: 200W

data_description.schedule_start: Time when scheduled charging window begins.
data_description.schedule_end: Time when scheduled charging window ends.
data_description.schedule_days: Days of the week when scheduled charging is active.
